page: Amazon Web Services t3.nano is a burstable, entry-level virtual machine instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It includes 0.5 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 0.25 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks place the t3.nano in the lower tiers of compute capability. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 1141.78, while the multi-core score reached 1661.39, reflecting its burstable CPU allocation model. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is measured at 93.39 GB/sec. Real-world utility benchmarks show a static web serving throughput of 1.72 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 1.5 million operations per second. These metrics indicate that while the CPU can burst to handle brief spikes, sustained heavy workloads will deplete CPU credits and degrade performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3.nano represents a highly economical tier designed for low-utilization workloads where continuous full CPU performance is unnecessary. It is best suited for lightweight applications such as microservices, small development or staging environments, low-traffic static web servers, and basic administrative cron jobs.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.nano is an entry-level, burstable virtual machine instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 2 vCPUs and 0.5 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Benchmark results indicate limited compute capacity, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1661.39 and a static web serving throughput of 1.72 GB/sec, placing its performance in the lower percentiles. This resource profile makes the t3.nano a highly cost-efficient option for low-intensity, burstable workloads. It is best suited for lightweight tasks such as microservices, small development environments, low-traffic web serving, and basic background scripting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.nano is a burstable virtual instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 0.5 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, designed for low-intensity, budget-conscious workloads.

tagline: A highly economical, burstable x86_64 instance designed for low-utilization microservices, basic scripting, and lightweight development environments.
